title: Threshold
author: [Example — for CAP Narrative Profile conformance corpus]

interpretations.ghost: Her father died four months ago after years of emotional distance. She was not present at his death; she was in another city finishing a concert tour. The guilt has calcified into forward motion — keep moving, don't stop, don't feel.
interpretations.want: To sell the childhood home quickly, settle the estate, and return to her life in the city
interpretations.need: To stop moving long enough to grieve; to discover that her father's silence was not absence of love
interpretations.flaw: Compulsive forward motion; inability to be still with loss

interpretations._note: Robert is dead before the story begins. He is present only through objects (the house, the garden, the journal) and Nadia's memory. His arc is flat in the sense that he cannot change — the change is Nadia's perception of him.

interpretations.description: A loving but emotionally inarticulate father; a daughter who learned his language of silence and resented it

want_vocabulary.escape: To leave the house and the grief behind
want_vocabulary.connection: To find evidence that her father loved her
want_vocabulary.closure: To complete the transaction of death — settlement, sale, farewell
